result.stdout:
Description:
  Tool for verifying MSIL code based on ECMA-335.

Usage:
  ILVerify <input-file-path>... [options]

Arguments:
  <input-file-path>  Input file(s)

Options:
  -r, --reference <reference>              Reference metadata from the specified assembly
  -s, --system-module <system-module>      System module name (default: mscorlib)
  -c, --sanity-checks                      Check for valid constructs that are likely mistakes
  -i, --include <include>                  Use only methods/types/namespaces, which match the given regular expression(s)
  --include-file <include-file>            Same as --include, but the regular expression(s) are declared line by line in the specified file.
  -e, --exclude <exclude>                  Skip methods/types/namespaces, which match the given regular expression(s)
  --exclude-file <exclude-file>            Same as --exclude, but the regular expression(s) are declared line by line in the specified file.
  -g, --ignore-error <ignore-error>        Ignore errors, which match the given regular expression(s)
  --ignore-error-file <ignore-error-file>  Same as --ignore-error, but the regular expression(s) are declared line by line in the specified file.
  --statistics                             Print verification statistics
  --verbose                                Verbose output
  -t, --tokens                             Include metadata tokens in error messages
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information
  -v, --version                            Show version information
